sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 1 completed! The solution was to move the player to rows 28-31, cols 24-43 (center of the board) and press SPACE.
Now I'm on level 2. Let me inspect the new level to understand the layout and goal.
Looking at the image, the layout is different:
- Yellow structures at the TOP (rows 0-7)
- Red blocks in the middle
- Blue player in the lower middle
- Magenta/charcoal blocks at the bottom-right
- Green timer at the BOTTOM
- Gray ground at the TOP
This is like an inverted version of level 1. The timer is at the bottom, the ground is at the top, and the yellow structures are at the top.
Let me inspect the current frame to understand the layout better.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: Level 1 completed! Solution: center player (rows 28-31, cols 24-43) + SPACE. Now on level 2 with inverted layout.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Level 2 objects:")
for n in seg:
    print(f"  {n['color']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}...")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Level 2 has:
- Light gray ground: rows 0-3 (top)
- Orange background: rows 4+
- Yellow structures: 3 of them at rows 4-11, cols 12-23, 28-39, 44-55
- Red blocks: 2 of them at rows 16-19, cols 8-19 and rows 24-27, cols 28-39
- Blue player: rows 36-39, cols 20-39
- Magenta block: rows 56-59, cols 40-43
- Charcoal block: rows 60-62, cols 40-43
- Green timer: row 63 (bottom)
This is indeed an inverted version of level 1:
- Level 1: timer at top, ground at bottom, yellow structures at bottom, charcoal/magenta at top-right
- Level 2: timer at bottom, ground at top, yellow structures at top, charcoal/magenta at bottom-right
The player is at rows 36-39, cols 20-39. The center of the board would be rows 30-33, cols 24-43 (approximately).
In level 1, the solution was to move the player to the center (rows 28-31, cols 24-43) and press SPACE.
Let me try the same approach: move the player to the center and press SPACE.
From the current position (rows 36-39, cols 20-39):
- To get to rows 28-31: UP 2
- To get to cols 24-43: RIGHT 1
Let me try UP 2, RIGHT 1, SPACE.
Wait, but the layout is different. In level 1, the player started at rows 16-19, cols 12-31 and needed to go to rows 28-31, cols 24-43 (DOWN 3, RIGHT 3).
In level 2, the player is at rows 36-39, cols 20-39. The center would be around rows 30-33, cols 24-43.
Let me calculate: from rows 36-39 to rows 30-33 is UP 1.5, which rounds to UP 2 (rows 28-31). From cols 20-39 to cols 24-43 is RIGHT 1.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The board is 64x64. The center is at rows 30-33, cols 30-33 (approximately). The player is 4 rows tall and 20 cols wide.
In level 1, the solution was rows 28-31, cols 24-43. The center of this position is row 29.5, col 33.5.
In level 2, if I use the same center, the player would be at rows 28-31, cols 24-43.
From the current position (rows 36-39, cols 20-39):
- UP 2: rows 28-31
- RIGHT 1: cols 24-43
Let me try this.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Level 2 has inverted layout. Player at rows 36-39, cols 20-39. 3 yellow structures at top, 2 red blocks, charcoal/magenta at bottom-right. Timer at bottom.
Plan: Try same strategy as level 1 - center player and press SPACE. Target: rows 28-31, cols 24-43.
